title: Address reuse on advance_diversifier_to failure
description: generate_fresh_address() in commands.rs: next_diversifier() succeeds and returns start index; Sapling skips invalid diversifiers (found_di > start); advance_diversifier_to(found_di + 1) is called and fails (DB error). Function returns PaymentAction::Unknown and exits. On the next /pay call, next_diversifier() returns the same start index again because the advancement never committed. The same Sapling subaddress is issued to a different payment session, linking two payments to the same on-chain identity. Breaks the fresh-subaddress-per-payment invariant and the legal 'no custody / no tracking' posture. The comment at lines 1638–1640 acknowledges the risk but provides no mitigation.
close_reason: False positive. advance_diversifier_to failure propagates via ? — Ok(addr) is never reached on failure so the address is never issued. The next call finds the same found_di and issues it for the first time. No reuse possible. Comment in code is slightly misleading but behavior is correct.
